titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Bernstein Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 4, 2019 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 23-24 (printed email): Restricted: Not to be consulted before January 1, 2033.
Box 52 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 53 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc., on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Writings and Correspondence, 1980-2010. II. Notebooks, 1989-2010. III. Other Papers, 1950-2012. IV. Audiovisual Materials, circa 1970s-1980s. V. Electronic Media, 1990s-2010s. VI. Printed Materials, 1970-2010.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Bernstein (1950-) is an American poet, essayist, and scholar. He is a prominent figure among the Language poets and taught for many years at the University of Pennsylvania. Bernstein was awarded the 2019 Bollingen Prize for American Poetry.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Bernstein Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Charles Bernstein papers, which span from 1950-2010s, contain extensive writings, along with other papers, programs, clippings, notebooks, audiovisual materials, and electronic media that document or are related to Bernstein's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Charles Bernstein Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain arrangement found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, correspondence, and notes by or related to Charles Bernstein.

did.unittitle: Typescripts, correspondence, drafts, notes and other materials

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.p: This series contains notebooks containing writings by or related to Charles Bernstein.

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal papers, notes, clippings, posters, and ephemera notes related to Charles Bernstein's life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 52 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audiovisual materials related to Charles Bernstein's life and work.

did.materialspec: Static and a high amount of hum can be heard on the tape. The program is distorted at times.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents on this audiotape reel are included below.

list.item: 1. Praised Be the Lord to Home All Praise is [Due]
2. Called Upon Us and Given [They] Law
5. Yimcock and Hab Har Ba Torah

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. A warble in the aduio can be heard at about 17:45. Drops in the audio can be heard throughout the audiotape reel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains serials and books related to Charles Bernstein, his writings, and his research.
